item.title: Floater pioneer takes hands-on approach to solve the 'chicken and egg' problem
item.description: France's top floating wind technology sees calculated risk-taking and some firm choices as the route to serialised European production of floater

item.title: 'This war is a reckoning for our energy system – the green solution is clear'
item.description: Large scale wind and solar farms producing green hydrogen and ammonia are the only credible path to both fuel and climate security, writes Alex Tancock

item.title: The surprise market that's trouncing the EU on energy storage
item.description: Turkey has approved 33GW of battery storage projects, compared to around 12-13GW in Germany and Italy

item.title: Why Europe’s biggest power users are feeling frustrated
item.description: Flexibility and grid expansion are all very well, says European Metals chief James Watson, but not if his members end up paying

item.title: The business plan behind 'the world's biggest battery' – and its seeds at Tesla
item.description: Form Energy has set the energy storage industry buzzing with a trio of big deals in 2026 – its CEO tells Recharge that scale was always the plan

item.title: Deadline looms for Pentagon response on US wind permitting delays
item.description: The Defence Department is holding up 7.5GW of new capacity without explanation, renewables advocate American Clean Power Association said in a letter last month

item.title: US anti-offshore wind groups waffle on Trump oil & gas support
item.description: Industry opponents praise President for his relentless attacks but other actions including exempting offshore oil & gas from key environmental protections are raising concerns

item.title: Inside the 'baffling' mindset of US offshore wind opponents
item.description: Industry opponents praise President for his relentless attacks but other actions including exempting offshore oil & gas from key environmental protections are raising concerns

item.title: Democrats write to Total chief slamming 'outrageous' Trump offshore wind deal
item.description: House Democrats demand answers over how deal to quit US offshore wind farm projects and not come back was struck, and the legal basis for it

item.title: EU windfall tax could ‘endanger’ investments warns WindEurope
item.description: The European wind industry lobby thinks making interventions to the market is ‘unjustified’ because the current situation is not as severe as in 2022

item.title: How insurers adapted after 'death by a thousand cuts' from offshore wind
item.description: Insurers are now finding right balance after being burned by low cost, high frequency breakdowns, says head of renewables at Tokio Marine GX

item.title: Class action lawsuit filed against US battery firm
item.description: At least four law firms have issued alerts, alleging Eos Energy committed securities fraud

item.title: Pioneering UK floating wind project has contract terminated
item.description: UK chief of Mingyang says 'fact speaks for itself' after planned array it had preferred supplier agreement for has offtake deal terminated weeks after Chinese supplier barred from British projects

item.title: Dutch to stage double 2026 offshore wind tender volume 'in the face of geopolitical unrest'
item.description: Volume boost is an early statement of intent by new government led by pro-offshore wind Prime Minister Rob Jetten

item.title: German offshore wind operators obliged to install surveillance radars
item.description: Radar systems at strategic points in North and Baltic Seas slated to detect suspicious ships and drones

item.title: BP's new all-female top two means the renewables dream team is history
item.description: Deputy CEO appointment means three top roles at oil giant now switched from trio that took it deep into renewables

item.title: Mingyang taps Vattenfall for latest senior hire in Europe
item.description: Leading Chinese supplier of offshore wind turbines has been busy building top team in Europe with recent hires from Siemens Gamesa and others

item.title: Can Siemens Gamesa and Vestas meet Europe's offshore wind needs alone?
item.description: Europe’s annual deployment of offshore wind farms expected to triple over next decade, with analysts concerned about dependency on just two turbine suppliers

item.title: US to merge offshore energy regulators split by BP Deepwater Horizon disaster
item.description: Move would reintegrate law enforcement and industrial promotion, which were separated by Obama due to conflicts of interest that led to the worst oil spill in US history

item.title: Wind turbine giant to invest over $10bn in green fuels projects
item.description: Mingyang, a leading manufacturer of wind turbines and other clean power technologies, is yet to comment on announcement from Ethiopian Investment Commission

item.title: France combines offshore wind tenders to create huge auction round
item.description: 10GW round for fixed and floating will reportedly set limits on China-made content in bid to protect European supply chain

item.title: Five key lessons for a data centre and energy win-win
item.description: Big ambitions in AI and data can't succeed if they're out of step with the infrastructure needed to power them, writes Adrian Del Maestro

item.title: Euro MP fears for renewables investments if 'EU patriots' keep lining Trump's pockets
item.description: MEP Nicolás González Casares says key mechanisms such as ETS must be protected as Iran war exposes energy policy fractures

item.title: Mingyang: 'UK has not ruled out our Scottish factory'
item.description: UK government has said Mingyang turbines are not welcome in British offshore wind farms – but supplier insists that is not the final word on its Scottish factory

item.title: Masdar and TotalEnergies to merge wind and solar operations
item.description: UAE green power group and oil giant to create $2.2bn joint venture in Asia

item.title: Data centres just took the football test: for power systems the result was 'profound'
item.description: As grids struggle to cope with demand for connections, a start-up backed by Nvidia, GE Vernova, Siemens and a host of other big names hopes to help rewrite the rules over how massive data centres interact with power networks

item.title: Data centre power took the 'football test': for grids the result was 'profound'
item.description: As grids struggle to cope with demand for connections, a start-up backed by Nvidia, GE Vernova, Siemens and a host of other big names hopes to help rewrite the rules over how massive data centres interact with power networks

item.title: Crown Estate chief talks option fees, wind wakes and GB Energy
item.description: Head of marine at seabed landlord believes new marine route map could be game-changer for leasing round, while discussing more thorny issues

item.title: Crown Estate chief talks option fees, wind wakes and GB Energy
item.description: Head of marine at seabed landlord believes new marine route map could be game-changer for leasing round, while discussing more thorny issues

item.title: Largest US grid faces two key market hurdles as milestone battery tech deploys
item.description: Transmission costs and interconnection barriers in the major US grid continue, but innovations such as RWE's milestone sodium-ion pilot battery could make a difference long-term
